title: Fridericia dichotoma
intro: Fridericia dichotoma, also known as Laxa, is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to relieve gastrointestinal issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Fridericia dichotoma include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Fridericia dichotoma include teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat digestive issues and other related health problems.
taxonomy: Fridericia dichotoma is classified in the family Bignoniaceae. This plant is a member of the tribe Bignonieae and the genus Fridericia.
